text:
The countdown is onto the2024 Paris Olympics, with the blockbuster Opening Ceremony set to see millions around the world tune in as it all kicks off.

WhenLondonhosted the Olympics in 2012, around 27 million people in the UK tuned in for the opening ceremony, with 900 million worldwide also watchingthe jaw-dropping spectacle.

This year will mark a century sinceFrancelast hosted the Olympics, which were also held in the French capital.

However, this year’s opening event isn’t a conventional one, with organisers shunning a stadium show for the first time and instead choosing to host the parade on the river Seine.

With 1.5 billion expected to tune in, Paris 2024 president Tony Estanguet has promised the ceremony will be ‘truly poetic’.

The celebrations will see the thousands ofathletes competing in the games(10,500 in total), which run from July 26 until August 11, travelling on over 100 boats down a 6-kilometre route on the iconic river and passing iconic landmarks including the Notre Dame and Louvre on their way to the Eiffel Tower.

Watching on willbe over 500,000 spectators on the banks of the Seine, however for those unable to be there, here’s everything you need to know about tuning in from home.

The Paris Olympics opening ceremony will take placetomorrow, Friday July 26, marking the start of the event, which runs until the closing ceremony on Sunday, August 11.

The event kicks off at 7.30pm in Paris, or 6.30pm UK time.

BBC will air the opening ceremony, with the national broadcaster’s coverage beginningat 5.45pm on BBC Oneand will run until 10pm.

The BBC’s coverage will be led by Clare Balding, Gabby Logan, Hazel Irvine, Isa Guha, Jeanette Kwakye, JJ Chalmers and Mark Chapman.

Wider Olympics coverage in the UK will be split over several channels, with BBC running free coverage from 8am to 10pm each day, switching to BBC Two during the news.

The BBC is also running an Olympics Extra channel on iPlayer that will show coverage from 8am to around 11pm.

However, the BBC won’t be showing all sports and all medals, with further coverage of the Paris 2024 Olympics on Eurosport 1 and Eurosport 2, as well as online with Discovery Plus, which require either a Sky TV membership or subscription to Discover Plus.

The full line-up for tomorrow’s opening ceremony is yet to be confirmed, however there’s been plenty of speculation as to who could be taking to the stage.

There’s beenrumours swirling thatCeline DionandLady Gagawill be part of the eventafter they were each spotted in Paris in recent days.

On Thursday, French journalist Thierry Moreau also said the pair were set to perform Édith Piaf’s classic La Vie en Rose together.

If this does happen, it will be the first time Celine has performed in four years after being diagnosed with stiff person syndrome.

The singer also already has a connection with the games, after performing The Power of the Dream at the opening ceremony of the Atlanta games in 1996.

Aya Nakamura, the most listened-to French singer worldwide, is also expected to perform.

title: What time is the Paris 2024 Olympics ceremony tonight? How to watch on TV | Metro News
description: The 2024 Olympics kicks off in Paris tomorrow – but who'll be performing at the opening ceremony? Find out, plus when and how to watch on TV
